name: Bosque del Sur Loop from La Tenería II
name_original: El Bosque del Sur desde La Tenería II — circular
description: This 3.4-mile (5.5 km) circular jogging route in Bosque del Sur from La Tenería II offers a moderate workout with 77 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Pinto traverse a landscape characterized by urban parks and green spaces, offering accessible paths for various running styles. The terrain generally features gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for recreational running. These routes often connect different park areas and local paths, providing a mix of natural and developed environments within the region. Best jogging routes around Pinto The most popular jogging route is Parque Juan Carlos I to Camino del Arenalejo — circular from Pinto, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path connecting a prominent park with a local track. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque Norte and Bosque del Sur Loop, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This loop takes runners through two significant green areas, providing varied scenery. Local runners also love the La Tenería I and II Loop, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through local residential areas and green spaces, often completed in about 38 minutes. Jogging around Pinto is defined by urban parks, local paths, and green corridors. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate runs. The routes in Pinto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 360 runners have used komoot to explore Pinto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Pinto?
A: Pinto offers a diverse selection of over 35 jogging routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes traverse urban parks, local paths, and green corridors, providing a mix of natural and developed environments.

Q: Are there easy running paths suitable for beginners or families in Pinto?
A: Yes, Pinto has several easy running paths. While most routes are rated moderate, there is at least one easy route available, and many moderate routes feature gentle elevation changes, making them suitable for recreational running and families. For a moderate option that is popular, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243334183/la-teneria-i-and-ii-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tenería I and II Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.1 km) long.

Q: What are some challenging running trails in Pinto?
A: For those seeking a more challenging run, Pinto offers 4 routes rated as difficult. These trails will provide more significant elevation changes or longer distances to test your endurance within the region's diverse landscape.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Pinto?
A: Yes, many of Pinto's jogging routes are circular, offering convenient loops that bring you back to your starting point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243294352/parque-juan-carlos-i-to-camino-del-arenalejo-circular-from-pinto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Juan Carlos I to Camino del Arenalejo — circular from Pinto&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.8-mile (11.0 km) trail connecting a prominent park with a local track.

Q: What kind of scenery or attractions can I expect along the running routes in Pinto?
A: Jogging routes in Pinto offer a mix of urban green spaces and natural elements. You can enjoy views of local parks like Parque Juan Carlos I and Parque Norte, as well as green corridors. For scenic viewpoints, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3111645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Madrid from Cerro de Buenavista&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus of Cerro de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on routes slightly outside the immediate town center.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Pinto?
A: The running community highly rates Pinto's trails, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the accessibility of the urban parks and green spaces, the varied scenery, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience. Over 360 runners have already explored Pinto's terrain using komoot.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through multiple parks?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243312989/parque-norte-and-bosque-del-sur-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Norte and Bosque del Sur Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, taking runners through two significant green areas for varied scenery and a longer run of 7.5 miles (12.1 km).

Q: Can I find routes that offer a tour of the town center?
A: Yes, if you're interested in combining your run with a glimpse of the town, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243348223/tour-of-the-center-of-pinto-from-parque-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the center of Pinto from Parque Europa&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This 8.5-kilometer route allows you to jog through local areas starting from Parque Europa.

Q: Are there any routes that focus on the Bosque del Sur area?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy the Bosque del Sur, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243408954/bosque-del-sur-loop-from-la-teneria-ii" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque del Sur Loop from La Tenería II&lt;/a&gt; offers a dedicated 5.5-kilometer path through this green space, providing a pleasant and accessible running experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Pinto?
A: Pinto's climate generally allows for jogging year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities, with spring bringing wildflowers and autumn showcasing rich red and golden hues in some areas. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are often preferred, while winters are generally mild enough for daytime jogging with appropriate attire.
tips: El Bosque del Sur is a large green area perfect for jogging or just having a picnic. It's like a great green belt for Pinto.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 5.5 kilometers long. Most people complete it in about 35-40 minutes, depending on their pace.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is generally easy, suitable for jogging. It features minimal elevation changes, making it accessible for most fitness levels. You'll find yourself on paths that are well-suited for a comfortable run.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, with its moderate difficulty and minimal elevation gain, this route is quite suitable for beginners and casual joggers. It's a great option for a regular exercise routine without significant challenges.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route originating from the La Tenería II area. While specific trailhead parking isn't designated in the data, as it's a residential area, you would typically find street parking nearby. Public transport options to La Tenería II in Pinto, Madrid, are also available.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the Bosque del Sur de Pinto, offering a pleasant green space for your jog. While it's not the extensive natural park of the same name in Jaén, it provides a nice local forest environment.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, local parks and green spaces like the Bosque del Sur de Pinto are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access this area?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any permits or to pay an entrance fee to jog this route. It's a publicly accessible green space within the Pinto area, not part of a protected natural park requiring special access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: This route can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful foliage. In summer, early mornings or late evenings are best to avoid the heat, while winter jogs are comfortable on clear days.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other local paths. You will jog along parts of the Camino Viejo de Madrid and Calle de Eduardo Chillida during your circular journey.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the route is located near the residential area of La Tenería II in Pinto, Madrid, you should find various amenities such as cafes, shops, and other services within a short distance of the starting point.

faqs.question: Is this trail part of the challenging 'Bosque del Sur' trails in Jaén?
faqs.answer: No, this specific jogging route, 'The Bosque del Sur from La Tenería II — circular,' is a local trail located in Pinto, Madrid. It is distinct from the demanding, mountainous 'Bosque del Sur' trails found within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park in Jaén, which are known for ultra-trail running and challenging hikes.
